title: hackelia sessilifructa
intro: Hackelia sessilifructa, also known as blue lace flower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the borage family and is native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of blue lace flower include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
